G7000x|notes_from_pm.value: TOMB OF HETEPHERES [I]. Temp. Khufu. Husband, Snefru. Son, Khufu. No superstructure. Re-burial, transferred from unidentified tomb probably at Dahshûr. Reisner Excavation. Harvard-Boston Expedition (1925-7).
G7000x|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-2 G 7000X headword block depth. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.179 (physical p.176) opens § B. EAST FIELD with `G 7000X. TOMB OF HETEPHERES [I]¹ / Temp. Khufu / Husband, Snefru. Son, Khufu. / No superstructure. Re-burial, transferred from unidentified tomb probably at Dahshûr. / Reisner Excavation. Harvard-Boston Expedition (1925-7). / REISNER and SMITH, Giza Necropolis, ii ...`. Per the chunk-2 prompt's headword-block rule, the block extends until the first bibliographic ribbon line (`REISNER and SMITH, ...`). Agent A's transcription is the longest faithful capture of this block (drops only the trailing `¹` footnote marker per the chunk-2 prompt's footnote-stripping rule); agents B and C each truncated short. Selecting A's content as the most PM-faithful capture of the full headword block.

LG84|occupant_alt_names.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-3 LG 84 PAKAP alternate-name normalisation. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.290 headword reads `LG 84. PAKAP good name WEHEBREc-EMAKHET Overseer of scribes of the King's repast, etc. Dyn. XXVI.`. The all-caps trailing-`c` (in pypdf) is PM's raised-ayin glyph for Late-Period Greek-derived throne-names like Wahibre (Apries) — same typographic class as chunk-1's `MENKAUREa` rendered with raised-`a` and resolved to `Menkaureʿ` (U+02BF). Three agents produced three different normalisations: `Wehebrec-Emakhet` (Agent A; kept the `c` literal + capitalised `Emakhet`), `Wehebrec-emakhet` (Agent B; kept `c` + lowercase post-hyphen), `Wehebreʿ-emakhet` (Agent C; converted `c` → U+02BF ayin + lowercase post-hyphen). Agent C's form is the most Egyptologically-faithful: the `c` is the raised-ayin and should be the canonical ayin glyph (matches chunk-1's `Menkaureʿ`); the post-hyphen `emakhet` is the locative `m-akhet` ("in the horizon"), not a proper name, so lowercase. Selecting Agent C.

SAQ-IputII|notes_from_pm.value: PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF IPUT II. Dyn. VI.
SAQ-IputII|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-4 SAQ-IputII headword block. PM III.2 2nd ed. 1978/1981 printed p.432 (physical p.72) prints `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF IPUT [II]¹` (regnal-number bracket + footnote marker) on a line followed by `Dyn. VI` on the next line. pypdf concatenated the lines and rendered `[II]¹` as `Il1`. Three agent forms: `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF IPUT II. Dyn. VI.` (Agent A; regnal-number normalised + sentence-final period), `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF IPUT. Dyn. VI` (Agent B; missed the regnal number entirely and missed the period), `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF IPUT II. Dyn. VI PYRAMID.` (Agent C; correct regnal + concatenated the next sub-heading `PYRAMID.` from the body block). Agent A is the most PM-faithful: regnal number rendered correctly per the chunk-4 bracket-regnal rule + sentence-final period preserved + headword block correctly bounded at the `Dyn. VI.` line (does NOT spill into the body's `PYRAMID.` sub-heading).

SAQ-Neit|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-4 SAQ-Neit headword block. PM III.2 2nd ed. 1978/1981 printed p.431 (physical p.71) prints `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF NEIT` on a line followed by `Dyn. VI` on the next line, then `PYRAMID.` as a body sub-heading. Three agent forms: `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF NEIT. Dyn. VI.` (Agent A; sentence-final period preserved), `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF NEIT. Dyn. VI` (Agent B; missed the period), `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF NEIT. Dyn. VI PYRAMID.` (Agent C; concatenated the next body sub-heading). Same pattern as SAQ-IputII: Agent A is the most PM-faithful — sentence-final period preserved + headword block correctly bounded at `Dyn. VI.` line. Selecting A.

SAQ-MerenreI|notes_from_pm.value: I. PYRAMID-COMPLEX OF MERENREʿ I. Dyn. VI. PYRAMID. Lepsius, XXXIX; Perring and Vyse, 8.
SAQ-MerenreI|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-4 SAQ-MerenreI headword block. PM III.2 2nd ed. 1978/1981 printed p.425 prints `MERENRĒʿ I` (macron-ē + raised-ayin); pypdf rendered the macron-ē-plus-ayin cluster as `£c`. The conventional Egyptological transcription is `MERENREʿ I` (restoring the `E` vowel of the `Re` element; parallel to chunk-1's `Menkaureʿ`). Three agent normalisations: `MERENREʿ I` (Agent A; restored the `E`), `MERENRʿ I` (Agents B and C; strict-rule dropped the vowel). Selecting Agent A's form per egyptologist-reviewer F1 P1 — the `Merenreʿ I` conventional form is the Egyptological standard. The parallel `fix_rows.py` CHUNK4_CORRECTIONS entry restores the `occupant_name` to `Merenreʿ I` so all fields stay internally consistent. Also picks `Dyn. VI. PYRAMID. ...` (with period before PYRAMID) over Agent C's concatenated form.

G1151|notes_from_pm.value: Overseer of the department of tenants of the Great House, Prophet of Khufu: Prophet of Reʿ in the Sun-temple of Neuserreʿ, etc. End of Dyn. V or later.
G1151|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-6 G 1151 KEDNUFER headword block. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.56 (physical p.53) prints `G 1151. KEDNUFER Overseer of the department of tenants of the Great House, Prophet of Khufu: Prophet of Reʿ in the Sun-temple of Neuserreʿ, etc. End of Dyn. V or later.`. Three agent variants: (A) colon `Khufu:` + properly normalised ayin `Reʿ`/`Neuserreʿ`; (B) semicolon `Khufu;` + ayin normalised; (C) colon + un-normalised `Rea`/`Neuserrea` (raised-`a` left literal). The pypdf text-layer extraction shows COLON (verified at line 303 of chunk-6 text file), so agents A and C have the correct punctuation; the chunk-wide ayin-normalisation rule (raised-`a` → U+02BF) means agent A's `Reʿ`/`Neuserreʿ` is the correct normalisation per the source-wide convention established in chunks 1 + 3 + 5 (Menkaureʿ, Wehebreʿ-emakhet, Userkaf-aliases etc.). Agent A's form is the only one matching BOTH the text-layer punctuation AND the source-wide ayin convention. Selecting A.

G2100a|notes_from_pm.value: King's son of his body, Greatest of the seers in On, Treasurer of the God in the ba-neteru, bar-neteru, neb-rekhyt, and dua-tawy boats, etc. Temp. Menkaureʿ or Shepseskaf (Reisner) or early Dyn. V (Junker).
G2100a|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-7 G 2100-I-annexe (Merib's annexe to G 2100) headword block. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 prints Menkaureʿ with a raised-ayin glyph (parallel to chunk-1's `MENKAUREa` → `Menkaureʿ` ayin-normalisation). Three agent variants: (A) keeps raised-`a` literal (`Menkaurea`); (B) normalises to U+02BF (`Menkaureʿ`) per the source-wide convention; (C) raised-`a` literal + extends with `Stone-built mastaba. LG 24.` clause. Selecting B — matches the chunks 1-6 raised-ayin normalisation discipline (U+02BF). The Stone-built mastaba / LG 24 alias from C should land in `tomb_aliases` separately, not as a notes extension.
G2100a|notes_from_pm._note: LG 24 alias not committed to tomb_aliases pending egyptologist review verification of PM's headword vs body scope.

G2156b|notes_from_pm.value: Dyn. VI. Mastaba south of tomb G 2220. There is another G 2156 east of G 2155 (see p. 79).
G2156b|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-7 G2156b (= second PM `G 2156`, Redenes; renamed from `G2156` to `G2156b` per the chunk-7 duplicate-disambiguation pre_merge rule). The Redenes G 2156 is PM's second use of the same Reisner number for a different physical mastaba (south of G 2220), with PM cross-referencing the first G 2156 (Kanenesut II, east of G 2155 on p. 79). Three agent variants on the cross-reference clause: (A) full clause with `(see p. 79)` page ref; (B) clause without page ref; (C) drops the cross-reference entirely. Agent A's form is the most PM-faithful: preserves the verbatim cross-reference page ref. Selecting A.
G2156b|notes_from_pm._note: Pre_merge mechanism for the G2156→G2156b rename is documented in the chunk-7 transcribe.md scope note.

G1221|notes_from_pm.value: Shad (?) (?), Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.
G1221|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-6 G 1221 SHAD headword block. The pypdf text-layer extraction (chunk-6 file line 432) reads `G 1221. SHAD (?) (?), Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.` — the doubled `(?) (?)` is an OCR-stutter artifact, NOT what PM prints (printed-source verification: PM III.1 p.59 prints `SHAD (?)` once, per the egyptologist-reviewer pass). At merge time, before the egyptologist had verified the printed source, the three agent variants were: (A) `Shad (?) (?), Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.` (full text-layer-verbatim form, preserving the stutter); (B) `Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.` (dropped the name); (C) `(?) (?), Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.` (kept the hedges, dropped the name). Selecting A at merge time as the most text-layer-faithful form (captures the candidate name). The subsequent fix_rows.py CHUNK6_CORRECTIONS entry then trims to `Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.` per the egyptologist's recommended form once printed-source verification confirmed the OCR stutter. This two-step path makes the OCR/printed-source delta auditable.

G1201|notes_from_pm.value: King's son, Prophet of Heqet, heka-priest of Meḥyt, etc. Temp. Khufu.
G1201|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-6 G 1201 WEPEMNEFERT headword block. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.57 (physical p.54) prints `G 1201. WEPEMNEFERT King's son, Prophet of Heqet, heka-priest of Meḥyt, etc. Temp. Khufu.` — only Meḥyt (the cobra-goddess of Thinis, transliterated mḥyt) gets the underdot-Ḥ; Heqet (the frog-goddess, transliterated ḥqt) appears in PM's printed text without the diacritic per the volume's typesetting convention. The pypdf text-layer renders Meḥyt's underdot-Ḥ glyph as a mid-word uppercase `H` (`MeHyt`), parallel to chunk-3's underdot-glyph rendering issues. Three agent variants: (A) `Meḥyt` (correctly normalised to underdot-Ḥ); (B) `Mehyt` (dropped the underdot, lossy); (C) `MeHyt` (left the raw OCR uppercase-H, non-canonical). Agent A is the Egyptologically-faithful transcription matching PM's printed glyph. Selecting A. Pattern parallels chunk-3 LG 84's `WEHEBREc-EMAKHET` → `Wehebreʿ-emakhet` underdot normalisation. Egyptologist printed-source pass verified the asymmetric Heqet (no diacritic) vs Meḥyt (with diacritic) treatment matches PM's actual typesetting.

G2347a|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-8 G 2347a notes_from_pm. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.84 (physical p.81) prints `G 2347a.` on its own line as a bare-suffix headword (no occupant name, no title cluster, no dating marker), immediately followed by sub-feature heading `Shaft C.` Per the chunk-8 prompt's Shape-2 rule, pure-numeric (or pure-suffix) bare-headword rows with no dating marker get `notes_from_pm: null`. Three agent variants: (A) `G 2347a.` (incorrectly transcribed the tomb_id line itself into notes); (B) `G 2347a. Shaft C.` (included the next sub-feature heading, out of headword scope); (C) `null` (correctly identified bare-suffix → null per Shape-2 rule). Selecting C as the only form matching the chunk-8 prompt's documented Shape-2 normalisation.

G2381|notes_from_pm.value: Overseer of all works of the King, King's architect and builder in the Two Houses, Overseer of the royal commissions of the Pyramid of Pepy I, etc. Temp. Pepy I or Merenreʿ I. Father (possibly), Khnementi (tomb G 2374). Stone-built mastaba. Almost completely destroyed.
G2381|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-8 G 2381 MERYREʿ-MERYPTAḤʿANKH headword block. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.86–87 (physical p.86–87) prints the headword `G 2381 with shaft G 2382A. MERYREa-MERYPTAHaANKH good name NEKHEBU Overseer of all works of the King, King's architect and builder in the Two Houses, Overseer of the royal commissions of the Pyramid of Pepy I, etc. Temp. Pepy I or Merenrea I.` followed by `Father (possibly), Khnementi (tomb G 2374). Stone-built mastaba. Almost completely destroyed.` Three agent variants: (A) ayin-normalised `Merenreʿ I`, drops the `with shaft G 2382A.` structural-ID prefix (consistent with chunks 6–7 convention of placing structural identifiers in tomb_id/shared_with_tombs not notes); (B) literal raw `Merenrea I` (un-normalised raised-ayin); (C) ayin-normalised but prepends `With shaft G 2382A.` to notes. Selecting A — matches the chunk-wide ayin normalisation rule (raised-`a` → U+02BF) AND the chunks 6–7 convention that structural shaft-attachment prefixes don't appear in notes_from_pm. The shaft G 2382A relationship is preserved structurally (parent G2381 emits the row; shaft does not).

G2381|occupant_name.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-8 G 2381 occupant_name. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 prints `MERYRĒC-MERYPTAḤCANKH` (all caps, macron-Ē on the Re sun-god root, raised-`a`/`c` glyph for ayin both times — egyptologist PR #227 P1-4 verified macron-Ē against PM PDF p.86). Three agent normalisations: (A) `Meryreʿ-Meryptahʿankh` (capital post-hyphen `M`, NO underdot-Ḥ, NO macron); (B) `Meryreʿ-meryptaḥʿankh` (lowercase post-hyphen `m`, WITH underdot-Ḥ, NO macron); (C) `Meryreʿ-Meryptaḥʿankh` (capital post-hyphen `M`, WITH underdot-Ḥ, NO macron). Initial selection chose C (capital + underdot) over the egyptologically-canonical `Meryrēʿ-Meryptaḥʿankh` form. PR #227 round-1 egyptologist correction restores the macron-Ē per PM's printed typesetting (parallel to chunk-4 `Merenrēʿ I` macron preservation). Final value: capital post-hyphen `M` + underdot-Ḥ on the *ptḥ* root + macron-Ē on the Re component — all three PM-printed diacritics preserved.

G2387|occupant_name.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-8 G 2387 occupant_name — same underdot-Ḥ + post-hyphen-capital pattern as G 2381 (without the macron-Ē, since the Re sun-god root is not part of this name; the Pepy prefix has no long vowel). PM III.1 prints `PEPY-MERYPTAḤCANKH`. Three agent forms: (A) `Pepy-Meryptahʿankh` (no underdot), (B) `Pepy-meryptaḥʿankh` (lowercase + underdot), (C) `Pepy-Meryptaḥʿankh` (capital + underdot). Selecting C for the same Egyptological + chunk-7 reasons documented in the G 2381 occupant_name override entry.

G2415|notes_from_pm.value: and wife Meti. Late Dyn. V. Stone-built mastaba.
G2415|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-8 G 2415 WERI / METI joint-burial notes. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.96 (physical p.91 in chunk file) prints headword `G 2415. WERI and wife METI. Late Dyn. V.` followed by `Stone-built mastaba.` Three agent variants: (A) `And wife Meti. Late Dyn. V. Stone-built mastaba.` (capitalised `And` as if starting sentence after dropping `Weri`); (B) `and wife Meti. Late Dyn. V. Stone-built mastaba.` (lowercase `and` preserving the original conjunction); (C) `and wife. Late Dyn. V. Stone-built mastaba.` (dropped wife name `Meti`). Selecting B — most PM-faithful: preserves wife name AND preserves PM's lowercase `and` as the continuation conjunction (the chunk-8 convention drops only the OCCUPANT NAME `Weri` itself from notes since it lives in `occupant_name`; the rest of the headword wording is preserved verbatim). Wife name `Meti` is independently captured in `co_occupants` with role `Wife`, AND `is_joint_burial: true` per the explicit two-equal-occupant headword.

G2423|notes_from_pm.value: Judge and Elder of the Hall, Secretary of every secret judgement of the Great Court, Prophet of Maʿet, etc. Dyn. V-VI. Wife, Khenit. Stone-built mastaba.
G2423|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-8 G 2423 MEḤU headword block. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.97 (physical p.92 in chunk file) prints `G 2423. MEHU, Judge and Elder of the Hall, Secretary of every secret judgement of the Great Court, Prophet of Maaet, etc. Dyn. V-VI.` followed by `Wife, Khenit / Stone-built mastaba.` Three agent variants: (A) `... Prophet of Maʿet, etc. Dyn. V-VI. Wife, Khenit. Stone-built mastaba.` (ayin-normalised + preserves wife clause); (B) `... Prophet of Maaet, etc. Dyn. V-VI. Stone-built mastaba.` (raw OCR `Maaet` + drops wife clause); (C) `... Prophet of Maʿet, etc. Dyn. V-VI. Stone-built mastaba.` (ayin-normalised but drops wife clause). Selecting A — only variant satisfying BOTH (1) ayin normalisation `Maaet` → `Maʿet` (chunk-wide raised-`a` → U+02BF convention; goddess of truth/order Maʿet) AND (2) preservation of the wife attestation `Wife, Khenit.` which PM prints in the headword block before the `Stone-built mastaba` body cue. Wife `Khenit` is independently captured in `co_occupants` with role `Wife`.

value: Wife, Prophetess of Ḥathor Mistress-of-the-Sycamore and Neith Opener-of-the-Ways, etc.
G3008|co_occupant_roles.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-9 G 3008 SNEFRUḤOTP wife (Khenut called Inti) role string. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.95 (physical p.92) prints `Wife, Khenut called Inti Prophetess of Hathor Mistress-of-the-Sycamore and Neith Opener-of-the-Ways, etc.` Three agent variants: (A) `Wife, Prophetess of Ḥathor Mistress-of-the-Sycamore and Neith Opener-of-the-Ways, etc.` (underdot-Ḥ on Ḥathor; personal name `Khenut` in `co_occupants`, not in role; `etc.` trailer preserved); (B) `Wife, Prophetess of Hathor Mistress-of-the-Sycamore and Neith Opener-of-the-Ways, etc.` (no underdot; `co_occupants=['Khenut called Inti']` — packs alias into name field); (C) `Wife, called Inti, Prophetess of Hathor Mistress-of-the-Sycamore and Neith Opener-of-the-Ways` (inserts `called Inti` into role string; drops `etc.`). Selecting A — matches the chunk-8 PR #227 P1-3 Ḥathor underdot convention AND keeps the `co_occupant_roles` clean of personal names AND preserves PM's `etc.` trailer. Wife's beautiful name `Inti` is preserved verbatim in `notes_from_pm`.

G3098|notes_from_pm.value: with annexe. (a) Iymerery waʿb-priest of the King's mother, Inspector of waʿb-priests, Overseer of the dockyard, etc. Dyn. VI. Wife, Personet Royal acquaintance. (b) Neferḥetpes-Wer (woman), King's adorner, etc. Dyn. VI. Father, Duareʿ King's son of his body. North-east annexe.
G3098|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-9 G 3098 IYMERERY+NEFERHETPES-WER annexe-pair headword. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.99 (physical p.96). Synthesised value combines the egyptologically-faithful elements: (1) lowercase `with annexe.` (PM-verbatim, not capitalised), (2) primary occupant name `Iymerery` retained in (a) clause for parallel structure with (b)'s `Neferḥetpes-Wer`, (3) `waʿb-priests` ayin-normalised per source-wide raised-`a`→U+02BF convention, (4) `Neferḥetpes-Wer` underdot-Ḥ per chunk-8 PR #227 P1-3 Ḥathor/Meḥu convention, (5) `Duareʿ` ayin-normalised, (6) body trailer `Mastaba with stone filling lined with bricks.` dropped per chunks 6-7 mastaba-type-line exclusion.

JKR-Ithu|notes_from_pm.value: King's waʿb-priest. Dyn. VI. Parents (probably), Iaʿib Scribe, and Khaut (I) mitrt. Wife, Intkaes mitrt.
JKR-Ithu|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-10 JKR-Ithu notes. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.103 (physical p.100). Three agent variants differ on (1) ayin position in `Iaaib` (PM-raw OCR), (2) presence of `(I)` bracket disambiguator on Khaut, (3) mjtrt vs mitrt typography. Synthesised: (a) `Iaʿib` (raised-ayin between vowels — the second `a` of `Iaaib` is the ayin glyph, parallel to chunk-1 Menkaureʿ raised-ayin convention); (b) preserve `Khaut (I)` per PM-verbatim disambiguator; (c) `mitrt` per chunk-9 G 3050 normalisation. Agent C matches all three criteria — selecting C.

JKR-Ithu|co_occupant_roles.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-10 JKR-Ithu (PM `ITHU King's waab-priest`) co-occupant roles. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.103 (physical p.100) prints `mitrt. / Parents (probably), Iaaib Scribe, and Khaut (I) mitrt. Wife, Intkaes` — Ithu has THREE co-occupants in the headword block: parents Iaaib (Scribe) and Khaut I (mitrt) + wife Intkaes (mitrt, line-wrap from `mitrt.` on the prior line). Three agent variants: (A) `["mjtrt"]` (1 entry, raw OCR), (B) `["Parent (probably), Scribe", "Parent (probably), mitrt", "Wife, mitrt"]` (3-entry, all three co-occupants captured), (C) `["mitrt"]` (1 entry, normalised). Selecting B — only variant capturing the full parents-and-wife clause that PM prints. mitrt normalisation applied uniformly per chunk-9 G 3050 + chunk-10 JKR-Ankh/Irty precedents. Paired with co_occupants override above (3 names + 3 roles, length-coupled).

JKR-Irty|co_occupant_roles.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-10 JKR-Irty (PM `IRTY Recruit, Overseer of draughts-players of the crews, etc.`) wife role. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.100 (physical p.97). Same `Wife, mitrt` pattern as chunk-9 G 3050 + chunk-10 JKR-Ankh — selecting `["Wife, mitrt"]` for source-wide consistency.

JKR-Inpuhotp|notes_from_pm.value: Prophet of the shrine of Anubis in Aphroditopolis, Prophet of Saḥurēʿ and Neuserrēʿ, Prophet of Rēʿ in the Sun-temple of Neuserrēʿ, etc. Dyn. VI. Parents, Ither Prophet of Neuserrēʿ, etc., and Sabt Royal acquaintance. Wife, Senezem.
JKR-Inpuhotp|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-10 JKR-Inpuhotp (PM `INPUHOTP Prophet of the shrine of Anubis in Aphroditopolis`) notes. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.107 (physical p.104). The headword contains MULTIPLE Re-deity-compound royal names (Sahurēʿ, Neuserrēʿ, Rēʿ) where PM applies macron-Ē + ayin per the egyptological convention (parallel to chunk-4 Merenrēʿ I + chunk-8 Meryrēʿ). Sahurēʿ ALSO carries underdot-Ḥ on the *sḥ* root → `Saḥurēʿ` (egyptologically canonical form combining both diacritics). Three agent variants: (A) macron-Ē + ayin + final period, (B) underdot-Ḥ on Sahure + ayin BUT no macron + final period, (C) macron-Ē + ayin + no final period. Synthesised form: macron-Ē + ayin everywhere + underdot-Ḥ on Saḥurēʿ specifically + sentence-final period preserved. Combines the egyptologically-faithful elements of A and B.

JKR-SonbI|notes_from_pm.value: Director of dwarfs in charge of dressing, Tutor of the King's sons, Prophet of Khufu and Raʿzedef, etc. (dwarf). Middle Dyn. VI or later. Wife, Sentiotes I Prophetess of Ḥathor and Neith, etc.
JKR-SonbI|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-10 JKR-SonbI (the largest chunk-10 headword block — spans 3+ printed pages with chapel decoration sub-features but is ONE row per the chunk-10 single-row rule). PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed pp.101–103 (physical pp.98–100) prints the headword's wife clause with bracketed Roman regnal disambiguator `Sentiotes [I]` and underdot-Ḥ on Hathor. Three agent variants differ on (1) bracket form (`[I`, `[I]`, `[I],`), (2) Ḥathor underdot (A no, B yes, C no), (3) quote style. Synthesised form drops bracket per chunk-10 regnal rule (`Sentiotes I` — Roman appended with space), applies Ḥathor underdot per chunk-8/9 convention, ASCII straight quote.

JKR-Sinekhen|notes_from_pm.value: ʾtw-official, etc. Dyn. VI. Wife, Maʿkherui Female steward, etc.
JKR-Sinekhen|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-10 JKR-Sinekhen notes. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.105 (physical p.102) prints the OK title `ʾtw-official` (Egyptian *ʾtw*, transliterated with ʾ aleph); pypdf renders as `@`. PM also prints wife `Maʿkherui` with raised-ayin glyph; pypdf renders as `Maakherui`. Three agent variants: (A) ʾtw + Maʿkherui (both diacritics normalised), (B) @ + Maakherui (both raw OCR), (C) ʾtw + Maakherui (mixed). Selecting A — egyptologically-faithful in both elements per source-wide convention.

JKR-MeniII|co_occupant_roles.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-10 JKR-MeniII (PM `MENI [II]`) wife role. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.106 (physical p.103). Same `Wife` (no title cluster) pattern as JKR-Iiu — selecting `["Wife"]` per chunk-8 G 2415 + chunk-10 JKR-Iiu precedent for wives without explicit title.

JKR-Iiu|notes_from_pm.value: Overlord of linen (sšrw), etc. Dyn. VI. Wife, Tepemnefert.
JKR-Iiu|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-10 JKR-Iiu notes. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.106 (physical p.103) prints the Egyptian word for linen with italic *š* (hacek-s = transliteration of Egyptian s̆); pypdf renders italic-š as plain capital-S `sSrw`. Three agent variants: (A) `sšrw` (egyptologically-normalised + final period), (B) `sSrw` (raw OCR + final period), (C) `sSrw` (raw OCR, no final period). Selecting A — restores the Egyptological italic-š transliteration and preserves the PM sentence-final period.

JKR-Iiu|co_occupant_roles.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-10 JKR-Iiu (PM `IIU Overlord of linen`) wife role. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.106 (physical p.103) prints `Wife, Tepemnefert` with no title cluster following the name. Three agent variants: (A) `[""]` (empty string placeholder), (B) `["Wife"]` (chunk-8/9 G2415 Weri/Meti convention for wife without explicit title cluster), (C) `[]` (drops the wife entirely from co_occupant_roles, length-coupled to co_occupants). Selecting B — matches chunk-8 G 2415 precedent where the wife has no separate title cluster in PM.

JKR-Ankh|co_occupant_roles.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-10 JKR-Ankh (PM `ʿANKH ATw-official`) wife role. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.100 (physical p.97) prints `Wife, Nefertka, mjtrt.` Per chunk-9 G 3050 + G 3035 PR #228 egyptologist P3 normalisation (`mjtrt` and `mitrt` are PM publisher-typography variants of the same Egyptian OK female title; standardise on `mitrt`). Three agent variants: (A) `["mjtrt"]` (title only, raw typography), (B) `["Wife, mitrt"]` (chunk-9 normalised + `Wife,` prefix per chunk-8/9 convention), (C) `["mitrt"]` (normalised but no `Wife,` prefix). Selecting B — matches chunk-9 G 3050 convention `["Wife, mitrt"]` exactly.

value: King's adorner, etc. (woman, north-east annexe). Father, Duareʿ King's son of his body.
G3098|co_occupant_roles.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-9 G 3098 IYMERERY annexe-occupant role string. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.99 (physical p.96) prints G 3098 `with annexe.` containing TWO occupants: (a) IYMERERY waʿb-priest of the King's mother, Inspector of waʿb-priests, Overseer of the dockyard, etc. Dyn. VI; Wife, Personet Royal acquaintance. (b) NEFERHETPES-WER (woman), King's adorner, etc. Dyn. VI. Father, Duarea King's son of his body. North-east annexe. Per chunk-9 prompt's single-row annexe rule (distinct from chunk-7 G 2100-I-annexe Roman-numeral-suffixed pattern), G 3098 emits ONE row with both occupants captured via co_occupants/co_occupant_roles. Three agent variants on the annexe-occupant role: (A) bracketed `(north-east annexe; woman)` mid-string; (B) verbose form with embedded `Dyn. VI.` dating marker; (C) bracketed `(in north-east annexe)` + non-ayin `Duarea`. Selecting synthesized form: title cluster + parenthetical annexe-position + father lineage. Drops the embedded `Dyn. VI.` marker (lives in row's `dynasty` field) and ayin-normalises Duareʿ per the source-wide convention.

G3093|notes_from_pm.value: Supervisor of directors of the expedition, etc. Dyn. VI. Wife, Khnemt Prophetess of Neith Opener-of-the-Ways, and of Ḥathor Mistress-of-the-Sycamore, etc.
G3093|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-9 G 3093 MEDEDI notes. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.98–99 (physical p.95–96). Ḥathor + etc. preservation per same chunk-9 G 3008/G 3050 pattern. The third variant's body-trailer `Mastaba with stone filling lined with bricks.` is the structural sub-feature line (not part of headword block) and is dropped.

value: Wife, Prophetess of Neith Opener-of-the-Ways, and of Ḥathor Mistress-of-the-Sycamore, etc.
G3093|co_occupant_roles.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-9 G 3093 MEDEDI wife role. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.96 prints `Wife, Prophetess of Neith Opener-of-the-Ways, and of Hathor Mistress-of-the-Sycamore, etc.` Three agent variants differ only on (a) Ḥathor underdot and (b) `etc.` trailer preservation: (A) Ḥathor + etc., (B) Hathor + etc., (C) Hathor + no-etc. Selecting A — chunk-8 PR #227 P1-3 Ḥathor underdot convention + PM-verbatim `etc.` preservation.

G3050|notes_from_pm.value: ka-servant. Dyn. VI. Wife, Kednefert mitrt.
G3050|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-9 G 3050 ʿANKH notes. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.98 (physical p.95). Three agent forms: (A) `... Wife, Kednefert mjtrt.` (non-PM `j`-spelling), (B) `... Wife, Kednefert mitrt.` (PM-verbatim), (C) `... Wife, Kednefert mitrt. Brick-built mastaba with probably stone filling.` (PM-verbatim + body trailer). Selecting B — PM-verbatim title spelling per chunk-9 G 3050 co_occupant_roles parallel override, AND drops the mastaba-type body trailer per chunks 6-7 headword-block boundary convention (the `Brick-built mastaba...` line is the structural-type sub-feature, not part of the headword block).

G3050|co_occupant_roles.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-9 G 3050 ʿANKH wife role. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.95 prints `Wife, Kednefert mitrt.` — `mitrt` is an Old Kingdom female title (the female adjectival of *mitr*, conventionally translated 'witness' or 'sister-in-attendance'; cf. Edel, *Altäg. Grammatik* §271). Three agent variants: (A) `Wife, mjtrt` (uses `j` for the *i* phoneme — non-PM convention), (B) `Wife, mitrt` (PM-verbatim), (C) `Wife` (drops the title entirely). Selecting B — PM-verbatim preservation per the source-wide PM-faithful convention. The `mitrt` title is structurally a co-occupant role like `Royal acquaintance`, not a divine epithet, so the role string remains uncapitalized as PM prints it.
G3050|co_occupant_roles._note: Title preserved verbatim; downstream Egyptological normalisation (mitrt vs mjtrt vs mtrt) is out of scope for the PM-source extraction.

G3008|notes_from_pm.value: Inspector of waʿb-priests of the Pyramid of Snefru, Greatest of the Ten of Upper Egypt, etc. Dyn. VI. Wife, Khenut called Inti Prophetess of Ḥathor Mistress-of-the-Sycamore and Neith Opener-of-the-Ways, etc.
G3008|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-9 G 3008 SNEFRUḤOTP headword notes. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.95 (physical p.92). Three agent variants distinguish on three orthogonal axes: (axis 1) `wad-priests` (raw OCR-stutter for waʿb-priests, PM's `w3b`-priest title cluster) vs `waʿb-priests` (egyptologically-normalised form per chunk-1 Khufu-mortuary precedent and the source-wide raised-`a`→ayin convention); (axis 2) `Ḥathor` underdot-Ḥ vs plain `Hathor` (chunk-8 P1-3 Ḥathor convention from PR #227); (axis 3) inclusion vs exclusion of body trailer `Stone-built mastaba lined with bricks.` Combined: agent A has (Ḥathor + wad + no mastaba), agent B has (Hathor + wad + no mastaba), agent C has (Hathor + waʿb + with mastaba). Selecting a synthesis: `waʿb-priests` per chunk-1/source-wide ayin convention (agent C correct) + `Ḥathor` underdot per chunk-8 P1-3 convention (agent A correct) + drop body-trailer mastaba clause (chunks 6-7 convention: mastaba-type sub-feature line is NOT part of headword block, agents A+B correct). Final value combines the egyptologically-faithful element of each agent.
